Attribute.Name: Dimmer
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of a fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or brightness or in some cases even opacity shall be controlled with this attribute. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output. If a software makes use of an overall intensity control such as a grandmaster, it is important for the application to understand which attribute to change. In that scenario the dimmer attribute shall be the main intensity, brightness or opacity change.
Attribute.Label: Dimmer

Attribute.Name: Pan
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's sideward movement (horizontal axis).
Attribute.Explanation: PAN is one part of the movement that usually controls the horizontal movement. In regards to the fixture the Attribute of PAN will control the overall rotation around the basement of the fixture. If the fixture is mounted hanging it usually moves around the Z-axis. But PAN can also be used for virtual control of media server layers or other objects in virtual 3D environments. Usually PAN is used in relation with TILT.
Attribute.Label: Pan

Attribute.Name: Tilt
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's upward and the downward movement (vertical axis).
Attribute.Explanation: TILT is one part of the movement that usually controls the vertical movement .In regards to the fixture the Attribute of TILT will control the overall rotation around the yoke-axis of the fixture. If the fixture is mounted hanging it usually moves around the x-axis or y-axis, depending on the PAN. TILT can also be used for virtual control of media server layers or other objects in virtual 3D environments. Usually TILT is used in relation with PAN.
Attribute.Label: Tilt

Attribute.Name: PanRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed of the fixture's continuous pan movement (horizontal axis).
Attribute.Explanation: For fixtures with the ability to have a continuous pan rotation, the “Pan Rotate” control defines how fast (speed) a fixture can rotate about the pan axis. Usually, the horizontal axis.
Attribute.Label: Pan Rotate

Attribute.Name: TiltRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed of the fixture's continuous tilt movement (vertical axis).
Attribute.Explanation: For fixtures with the ability to have a continuous tilt rotation, the “Tilt Rotate” control defines how fast (speed) a fixture can rotate about the tilt axis. Usually, the vertical axis.
Attribute.Label: Tilt Rotate

Attribute.Name: PositionEffect
Attribute.Definition: Selects the predefined position effects that are built into the fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Effects which are built into the fixture, are based on movement and are of manufacturer specific nature so they cannot really be visualized are described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Position Effect

Attribute.Name: PositionEffectRate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed of the predefined position effects that are built into the fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Adjusting speed of some predefined movement effects is described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Position Effect Rate

Attribute.Name: PositionEffectFade
Attribute.Definition: Snaps or smooth fades with timing in running predefined position effects.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe functions that are adjusting fading or snapping behavior of some movement effects.
Attribute.Label: Position Effect Fade

Attribute.Name: XYZ_X
Attribute.Definition: Defines a fixture’s x-coordinate within an XYZ coordinate system.
Attribute.Explanation: Devices that can move straight in X-direction in space (opposite to pan or tilt circular movement) describe the movement with this attribute. A typical usage is left/right movement of a video-layer in space.
Attribute.Label: XYZ X

Attribute.Name: XYZ_Y
Attribute.Definition: Defines a fixture’s y-coordinate within an XYZ coordinate system.
Attribute.Explanation: Devices that can move straight in Y-direction in space (opposite to pan or tilt circular movement) describe the movement with this attribute. A typical usage is forward/backward movement of a video-layer in space.
Attribute.Label: XYZ Y

Attribute.Name: XYZ_Z
Attribute.Definition: Defines a fixture‘s z-coordinate within an XYZ coordinate system.
Attribute.Explanation: Devices that can move straight in Z-direction in space (opposite to distance of a pan/tilt device) describe the movement with this attribute. A typical usage is up/down movement of a video-layer in space.
Attribute.Label: XYZ Z

Attribute.Name: Rot_X
Attribute.Definition: Defines rotation around X axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Devices that can be rotated around the X-axis in space use this attribute. A typical usage is horizontal rotation of a video-layer in space (pitch axis).
Attribute.Label: Rot X

Attribute.Name: Rot_Y
Attribute.Definition: Defines rotation around Y axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Devices that can be rotated around the Y-axis in space use this attribute. A typical usage is rolling of a video-layer in space (roll axis).
Attribute.Label: Rot Y

Attribute.Name: Rot_Z
Attribute.Definition: Defines rotation around Z axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Devices that can be rotated around the Z-axis in space use this attribute. A typical usage is vertical rotation of a video-layer in space (yaw axis).
Attribute.Label: Rot Z

Attribute.Name: Scale_X
Attribute.Definition: Scaling on X axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that scales the percentage of movement on an X axis.
Attribute.Label: Scale X

Attribute.Name: Scale_Y
Attribute.Definition: Scaling on Y axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that scales the percentage of movement on an Y axis.
Attribute.Label: Scale Y

Attribute.Name: Scale_Z
Attribute.Definition: Scaling on Y axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that scales the percentage of movement on an Z axis.
Attribute.Label: Scale Z

Attribute.Name: Scale_XYZ
Attribute.Definition: Unified scaling on all axis.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that scales the percentage of movement on all X, Y and Z axes.
Attribute.Label: Scale XYZ

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)
Attribute.Definition: The fixture’s gobo wheel (n). This is the main attribute of gobo wheel’s (n) wheel control. Selects gobos in gobo wheel (n). A different channel function sets the angle of the indexed position in the selected gobo or the angular speed of its continuous rotation.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to select gobo in a gobo wheel for fixtures that provide gobo selection on one channel and gobo rotation/indexing on another channel(s). The actual gobo selection way - continuous or steps (when continuous, you can freely index the gobo wheel) is defined via physical properties of channel sets of this attribute. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Gobo

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)SelectSpin
Attribute.Definition: Selects gobos whose rotation is continuous in gobo wheel (n) and controls the angular speed of the gobo’s spin within the same channel function. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control selection and spin speed of gobos, with continuous rotation in gobo wheel, within the same channel function. When there is more than one gobo wheel a wild card (numerical identifier (n)) will be automatically added to indicate which wheel is being controlled. When using this Gobo(n)SelectSpin as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)SelectSpin as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Select Spin

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)SelectShake
Attribute.Definition: Selects gobos which shake in gobo wheel (n) and controls the frequency of the gobo’s shake within the same channel function.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control selection and shake speed of gobos, with gobo shake effect in gobo wheel, within the same channel function When using this Gobo(n)SelectShake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)SelectShake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Select Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This is the amount of shake as a percentage of the image size and defines the peak amplitude of the shake

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)SelectEffects
Attribute.Definition: Selects gobos which run effects in gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute for manufacturer defined fixture specific effects on a gobo wheel. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)SelectEffects as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)SelectEffects as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Select Effects

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)WheelIndex
Attribute.Definition: Controls angle of indexed rotation of gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to define indexed rotation angle of the gobo wheel for the gobos on the wheel When using this Gobo(n)WheelIndex as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)WheelIndex as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Index

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)WheelSpin
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed and direction of continuous rotation of gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Speed and direction of a continuous rotation of a gobo wheel ('gobo rainbow effect') is control with this attribute. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)WheelSpin as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)WheelSpin as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Spin

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)WheelShake
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of the shake of gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: For gobo wheel that provides wheel shaking function, this attribute allows to set the frequency of the shake. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)WheelShake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)WheelShake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This is the amount of shake as a percentage of the image size and defines the peak amplitude of the shake

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)WheelRandom
Attribute.Definition: Controls speed of gobo wheel´s (n) random gobo slot selection.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set the frequency of a random gobo selection. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)WheelRandom as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)WheelRandom as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Random

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)WheelAudio
Attribute.Definition: Controls audio-controlled functionality of gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to turn on audio based gobo functions, like random gobo selection in the beat of the music. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)WheelAudio as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n). In case that Gobo(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)WheelAudio as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Audio

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)Pos
Attribute.Definition: Controls angle of indexed rotation of gobos in gobo wheel (n). This is the main attribute of gobo wheel’s (n) wheel slot control.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set indexed position (angle) of a gobo in a gobo wheel and is typically used together with Gobo(n) attribute which selects the gobo. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Pos

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)PosRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed and direction of continuous rotation of gobos in gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set speed and direction of continuous rotation of a gobo in a gobo wheel and is typically used together with Gobo(n) attribute which selects the gobo. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)PosRotate as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n)Pos. In case that Gobo(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)PosRotate as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Pos Rotate

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)PosShake
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of the shake of gobos in gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set frequency of shaking of a gobo in a gobo wheel and is typically used together with Gobo(n) attribute which selects the gobo. If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Gobo(n)PosShake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Gobo(n)Pos. In case that Gobo(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Gobo(n)PosShake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Pos Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the peak amplitude of the shake

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)
Attribute.Definition: This is the main attribute of the animation wheel's (n) wheel control. Selects slots in the animation wheel. A different channel function sets the angle of the indexed position in the selected slot or the angular speed of its continuous rotation. Is used for animation effects with multiple slots. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to select one of the possible animators on an animation wheel for fixtures that provide animation selection on one channel and animation rotation/indexing on another channel(s). The actual animator selection way - continuous or steps (when continuous, you can freely index the animation wheel) is defined via physical properties of channel sets of this attribute. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)Audio
Attribute.Definition: Controls audio-controlled functionality of animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute controls how the animation wheel will behave when responding to an audio source for use with the AnimationWheel(n). When using this AnimationWheel(n)Audio as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n). In case that AnimationWheel(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)Audio as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Audio

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)Macro
Attribute.Definition: Selects predefined effects in animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows for selecting a fixture's predefined macro functions for the AnimationWheel(n). When using this AnimationWheel(n)Macro as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n). In case that AnimationWheel(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)Macro as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Macro

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)Random
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of animation wheel (n) random slot selection.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe random effects of the Animation wheel prepared by the manufacturer of the fixture. When using this AnimationWheel(n)Random as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n). In case that AnimationWheel(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)Random as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Random

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)SelectEffects
Attribute.Definition: Selects slots which run effects in animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe some effects of the AnimationWheel(n) prepared by the manufacturer of the fixture. When using this AnimationWheel(n)SelectEffects as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n). In case that AnimationWheel(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)SelectEffects as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Select Effects

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)SelectShake
Attribute.Definition: Selects slots which shake in animation wheel and controls the frequency of the slots shake within the same channel function.
Attribute.Explanation: When selecting a shake of an animation wheel slot and defining the frequency of the shake withing the same channel function, choose this attribute to describe the behavior. When using this AnimationWheel(n)SelectShake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n). In case that AnimationWheel(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)SelectShake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Select Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the peak amplitude of the shake

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)SelectSpin
Attribute.Definition: Selects slots whose rotation is continuous in animation wheel and controls the angular speed of the slot spin within the same channel function
Attribute.Explanation: When selecting a rotation of an animation wheel slot and defining the angular speed of the rotation withing the same channel function, choose this attribute to describe the behavior. When using this AnimationWheel(n)SelectSpin as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n). In case that AnimationWheel(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)SelectSpin as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Select Spin

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)Pos
Attribute.Definition: Controls angle of indexed rotation of slots in animation wheel. This is the main attribute of animation wheel (n) wheel slot control.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set indexed position (angle) of a animation wheel and is typically used together with AnimationWheel(n) attribute which selects the animation. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Pos

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)PosRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed and direction of continuous rotation of slots in animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set speed and direction of continuous rotation of an animation wheel and is typically used together with AnimationWheel(n) attribute which selects the animation. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this AnimationWheel(n)PosRotate as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n)Pos. In case that AnimationWheel(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)PosRotate as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Pos Rotate

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)PosShake
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of the shake of slots in animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set frequency of shaking of an animation wheel and is typically used together with AnimationWheel(n) attribute which selects the animation. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this AnimationWheel(n)PosShake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationWheel(n)Pos. In case that AnimationWheel(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationWheel(n)PosShake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Pos Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the peak amplitude of the shake

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)
Attribute.Definition: This is the main attribute of the animation system insertion control. Controls the insertion of the fixture's animation system in the light output. Is used for animation effects where a disk is inserted into the light output.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to select or engage an animation wheel for fixtures that provide animation selection on one channel and animation rotation/indexing on another channel(s). The actual animator selection way - continuous or steps (when continuous, you can freely index the animation wheel) is defined via physical properties of channel sets of this attribute. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Animation System

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)Ramp
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of animation system (n) insertion ramp.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)Ramp as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n). In case that AnimationSystem(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)Ramp as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Ramp

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the duration of the ramp in relation to the period.
This defines the minimum position in relation to the whole way of the spline
This defines the maximum position in relation to the whole way of the spline

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)Shake
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of animation system (n) insertion shake.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)Shake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n). In case that AnimationSystem(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)Shake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the minimum position in relation to the whole way of the spline
This defines the maximum position in relation to the whole way of the spline

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)Audio
Attribute.Definition: Controls audio-controlled functionality of animation system (n) insertion.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)Audio as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n). In case that AnimationSystem(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)Audio as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Audio

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)Random
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of animation system (n) random insertion.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)Random as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n). In case that AnimationSystem(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)Random as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Random

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)Pos
Attribute.Definition: This is the main attribute of the animation system spinning control. Controls angle of indexed rotation of animation system (n) disk.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set indexed position (angle) of a animation wheel and is typically used together with AnimationSystem(n) attribute which selects/engages the animation. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Pos

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)PosRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed and direction of continuous rotation of animation system (n) disk.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set speed and direction of continuous rotation of an animation wheel and is typically used together with AnimationSystem(n) attribute which selects/engages the animation. If there is more than one animation wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this AnimationSystem(n)PosRotate as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n)Pos. In case that AnimationSystem(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)PosRotate as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Pos Rotate

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)PosShake
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of the shake of animation system (n) disk.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)PosShake as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n)Pos. In case that AnimationSystem(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)PosShake as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Pos Shake

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the peak amplitude of the shake

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)PosRandom
Attribute.Definition: Controls random speed of animation system (n) disk.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)PosRandom as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n)Pos. In case that AnimationSystem(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)PosRandom as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Pos Random

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)PosAudio
Attribute.Definition: Controls audio-controlled functionality of animation system (n) disk.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this AnimationSystem(n)PosAudio as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to AnimationSystem(n)Pos. In case that AnimationSystem(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the AnimationSystem(n)PosAudio as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Pos Audio

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystem(n)Macro
Attribute.Definition: Selects predefined effects in animation system (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows for selecting a fixture's predefined macro functions for the AnimationSystem(n)
Attribute.Label: Animation System Macro

Attribute.Name: MediaFolder(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects folder that contains media content.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute selects one of multiple folders that (usually) contain media. Any folder selection process is possible with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Media Folder

Attribute.Name: MediaContent(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects file with media content.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute selects content that usually is located in one of the folders selected. Any content selection process is possible with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Media Content

Attribute.Name: ModelFolder(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects folder that contains 3D model content. For example 3D meshes for mapping.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is selects specifically one of multiple folders for 3D content. Any folder selection process is possible with this attribute. If there is more than one folder the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Model Folder

Attribute.Name: ModelContent(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects file with 3D model content.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute selects a 3D model that usually is located in one of the 3D folders selected. Any content selection process is possible with this attribute. If there is more than one content folder the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Model Content

Attribute.Name: Playmode
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow to set a mode of media playback, for example play and loop, play and stop..., use this attribute to describe this behavior.
Attribute.Label: Media Playmode

Attribute.Name: PlayBegin
Attribute.Definition: Defines starting point of media content playback.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to set the starting time of media content playback.
Attribute.Label: Media Play Begin

Attribute.Name: PlayEnd
Attribute.Definition: Defines end point of media content playback.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to set the end time of media content playback.
Attribute.Label: Media Play End

Attribute.Name: PlaySpeed
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts playback speed of media content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control the speed of media content playback
Attribute.Label: Media Play Speed

Attribute.Name: ColorEffects(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects predefined color effects built into the fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control selecting predefined color effects built into the fixture
Attribute.Label: Color Effects

Attribute.Name: Color(n)
Attribute.Definition: The fixture’s color wheel (n). Selects colors in color wheel (n). This is the main attribute of color wheel’s (n) wheel control.
Attribute.Explanation: Any device that can create colors by changing a mechanical color wheel inside the device uses this attribute to describe the different colors possible. If there is more than one color wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Color

Attribute.Name: Color(n)WheelIndex
Attribute.Definition: Controls angle of indexed rotation of color wheel (n)
Attribute.Explanation: The color wheel index describes an agle of rotation of the color wheel to exactly reproduce any mixture/position of the color wheel itself. The wild card will identify which color wheel will be indexed. When using this Color(n)WheelIndex as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Color(n). In case that Color(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Color(n)WheelIndex as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Index

Attribute.Name: Color(n)WheelSpin
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed and direction of continuous rotation of color wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: For special effects of a continuous rotating color wheel this attribute controls the direction of ration and the speed of rotation. If there is more than one color wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Color(n)WheelSpin as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Color(n). In case that Color(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Color(n)WheelSpin as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Spin

Attribute.Name: Color(n)WheelRandom
Attribute.Definition: Controls frequency of color wheel´s (n) random color slot selection.
Attribute.Explanation: The device internal random color wheel selection frequency will be controlled by this attribute. If there is more than one color wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Color(n)WheelRandom as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Color(n). In case that Color(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Color(n)WheelRandom as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Random

Attribute.Name: Color(n)WheelAudio
Attribute.Definition: Controls audio-controlled functionality of color wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: The device internal audio-based color wheel selection frequency will be controlled by this attribute. If there is more than one color wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Color(n)WheelAudio as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Color(n). In case that Color(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Color(n)WheelAudio as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Audio

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_R
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's red emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Red color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Red Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_G
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's green emitters for direct additive color mixing
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Green color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Green Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_B
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's blue emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Blue color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Blue Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_C
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's cyan emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Cyan color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Cyan Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_M
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's magenta emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Magenta color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Magenta Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_Y
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's yellow emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Yellow color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Yellow Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_RY
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's amber emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Amber color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Amber Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_GY
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's lime emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Lime color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Lime Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_GC
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's blue-green emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Blue-Green color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Blue-Green Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_BC
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's light-blue emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Light Blue color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Light-Blue Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_BM
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's purple emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Purple (Deep Blue) color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Purple Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_RM
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's pink emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Pink color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Pink Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_W
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's white emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive White color/emitter with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: White Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_WW
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's warm white emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Warm White color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Warm-White Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_CW
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's cool white emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Cool White color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Cool-White Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorAdd_UV
Attribute.Definition: Controls the intensity of the fixture's UV emitters for direct additive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive UV color/emitter/flag with direct access to the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: UV Additive Direct

Attribute.Name: ColorSub_R
Attribute.Definition: Controls the insertion of the fixture's red filter flag for direct subtractive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of insertion or saturation of a subtractive Red color flag/filter with direct access to the color. The color is the resulting color when the filter is fully inserted. Physical values go from 0 = no insertion/no color, to 1 = full insertion/full color saturation.
Attribute.Label: Red Subtractive

Attribute.Name: ColorSub_G
Attribute.Definition: Controls the insertion of the fixture's green filter flag for direct subtractive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of insertion or saturation of a subtractive Green color flag/filter with direct access to the color. The color is the resulting color when the filter is fully inserted. Physical values go from 0 = no insertion/no color, to 1 = full insertion/full color saturation.
Attribute.Label: Green Subtractive

Attribute.Name: ColorSub_B
Attribute.Definition: Controls the insertion of the fixture's blue filter flag for direct subtractive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of insertion or saturation of a subtractive Blue color flag/filter with direct access to the color. The color is the resulting color when the filter is fully inserted. Physical values go from 0 = no insertion/no color, to 1 = full insertion/full color saturation.
Attribute.Label: Blue Subtractive

Attribute.Name: ColorSub_C
Attribute.Definition: Controls the insertion of the fixture's cyan filter flag for direct subtractive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of insertion or saturation of a subtractive Cyan color flag/filter/emitter with direct access to the color. The color is the resulting color when the filter is fully inserted. Physical values go from 0 = no insertion/no color, to 1 = full insertion/full color saturation.
Attribute.Label: Cyan Subtractive

Attribute.Name: ColorSub_M
Attribute.Definition: Controls the insertion of the fixture's magenta filter flag for direct subtractive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of insertion or saturation of a subtractive Magenta color flag/filter/emitter with direct access to the color. The color is the resulting color when the filter is fully inserted. Physical values go from 0 = no insertion/no color, to 1 = full insertion/full color saturation.
Attribute.Label: Magenta Subtractive

Attribute.Name: ColorSub_Y
Attribute.Definition: Controls the insertion of the fixture's yellow filter flag for direct subtractive color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of insertion or saturation of a subtractive Yellow color flag/filter/emitter with direct access to the color. The color is the resulting color when the filter is fully inserted. Physical values go from 0 = no insertion/no color, to 1 = full insertion/full color saturation.
Attribute.Label: Yellow Subtractive

Attribute.Name: ColorMacro(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects predefined colors that are programmed in the fixture's firmware.
Attribute.Explanation: Choose this attribute to control the selection of predefined fixed colors, like Color Presets or Swatchbook Colors. The individual colors are defined by linking wheel slots to channel sets. If there is more than one color macro the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which color macro will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Color Macro

Attribute.Name: ColorMacro(n)Rate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the time between Color Macro steps.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function which is setting the time or speed of the Color Macro steps.
Attribute.Label: Color Macro Rate

Attribute.Name: CTO
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's "Correct to orange" wheel or mixing system.
Attribute.Explanation: Choose this attribute to control a function that modifies the Color Temperature of a fixture's light output. CTO decreases the Color Temperature from its default. Physical values describe the resulting Color Temperature.
Attribute.Label: CTO

Attribute.Name: CTC
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's "Correct to color" wheel or mixing system.
Attribute.Explanation: Choose this attribute to control a function that modifies the Color Temperature of a fixture's light output. CTC either increases or decreases the Color Temperature from its default. Physical values describe the resulting Color Temperature.
Attribute.Label: CTC

Attribute.Name: CTB
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's "Correct to blue" wheel or mixing system.
Attribute.Explanation: Choose this attribute to control a function that modifies the Color Temperature of a fixture's light output. CTB increases the Color Temperature from its default. Physical values describe the resulting Color Temperature.
Attribute.Label: CTB

Attribute.Name: Tint
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's "Correct green to magenta" wheel or mixing system.
Attribute.Explanation: Choose this attribute to control a function that adjusts the Green/Magenta shift of a fixture's light output.
Attribute.Label: Tint

Attribute.Name: HSB_Hue
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's color attribute regarding the hue.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control the fixture's Hue (color)
Attribute.Label: HSB Hue

Attribute.Name: HSB_Saturation
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's color attribute regarding the saturation.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control a fixture's color Saturation (richness)
Attribute.Label: HSB Saturation

Attribute.Name: HSB_Brightness
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's color attribute regarding the brightness.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control a fixture's color Brightness
Attribute.Label: HSB Brightness

Attribute.Name: HSB_Quality
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's color attribute regarding the quality.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow to adjust quality of HSB colors, use this attribute to describe this feature.
Attribute.Label: HSB Quality

Attribute.Name: CIE_X
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's CIE 1931 color attribute regarding the chromaticity x.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a feature that sets the x parameter of chromaticity of CIE 1931 based color definition.
Attribute.Label: CIE X

Attribute.Name: CIE_Y
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's CIE 1931 color attribute regarding the chromaticity y.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a feature that sets the y parameter of chromaticity of CIE 1931 based color definition.
Attribute.Label: CIE Y

Attribute.Name: CIE_Brightness
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's CIE 1931 color attribute regarding the brightness (Y).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a feature that sets the brightness of chromaticity of CIE 1931 based color definition.
Attribute.Label: CIE Brightness

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Red
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's red attribute for indirect RGB color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Red color/emitter/flag, where the device is making internal adjustments of the color and the operator thus has an indirect control of the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Red Additive Indirect

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Green
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's green attribute for indirect RGB color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Green color/emitter/flag, where the device is making internal adjustments of the color and the operator thus has an indirect control of the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Green Additive Indirect

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Blue
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's blue attribute for indirect RGB color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Blue color/emitter/flag, where the device is making internal adjustments of the color and the operator thus has an indirect control of the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Blue Additive Indirect

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Cyan
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's cyan attribute for indirect CMY color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Cyan color/emitter/flag, where the device is making internal adjustments of the color and the operator thus has an indirect control of the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Cyan Additive Indirect

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Magenta
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's magenta attribute for indirect CMY color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive Magenta color/emitter/flag, where the device is making internal adjustments of the color and the operator thus has an indirect control of the color. Physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Magenta Additive Indirect

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Yellow
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's yellow attribute for indirect CMY color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Gradual (fading) change of intensity or saturation of an additive yellow color/emitter/flag, where the device is making internal adjustments of the color and the operator thus has an indirect control of the color. physical values go from 0 = no output, to 1 = full output/saturation.
Attribute.Label: Yellow Additive Indirect

Attribute.Name: ColorRGB_Quality
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's quality attribute for indirect color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function of the device that allows the operator to control the quality parameter of the device's color mixing.
Attribute.Label: Additive Indirect Quality

Attribute.Name: VideoBoost_R
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts color boost red of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to raise up intensity of red color in the media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Boost Red

Attribute.Name: VideoBoost_G
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts color boost green of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to raise up intensity of green color in the media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Boost Green

Attribute.Name: VideoBoost_B
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts color boost blue of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to raise up intensity of green color in the media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Boost Blue

Attribute.Name: VideoHueShift
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts color hue shift of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to set color hue shift of media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Hue Shift

Attribute.Name: VideoSaturation
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts saturation of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to set saturation of media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Saturation

Attribute.Name: VideoBrightness
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts brightness of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to set brightness of media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Brightness

Attribute.Name: VideoContrast
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts contrast of content.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to set contrast of media content.
Attribute.Label: Video Contrast

Attribute.Name: VideoKeyColor_R
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts red color for color keying.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that provides media content color keying by red color.
Attribute.Label: Video Key Color Red

Attribute.Name: VideoKeyColor_G
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts green color for color keying.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that provides media content color keying by green color.
Attribute.Label: Video Key Color Green

Attribute.Name: VideoColorKey_B
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that provides media content color keying by blue color.
Attribute.Label: Video Color Key Blue

Attribute.Name: VideoKeyIntensity
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts intensity of color keying.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that adjusts the intensity of color keying.
Attribute.Label: Video Key Intensity

Attribute.Name: VideoKeyTolerance
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts tolerance of color keying.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that adjusts the tolerance value of color keying.
Attribute.Label: Video Key Tolerance

Attribute.Name: StrobeDuration
Attribute.Definition: Controls the length of a strobe flash.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that can adjust the duration of an individual flash.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Duration

Attribute.Name: StrobeRate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the time between strobe flashes.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that can adjust the time between individual flashes.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Rate

Attribute.Name: StrobeFrequency
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of strobe flashes.
Attribute.Explanation: Setting the frequency of strobing is described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Frequency

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeShutter
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode shutter. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a Shutter Mode.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Shutter

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeStrobe
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode strobe. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a Strobe Mode. When using this StrobeModeStrobe as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModeStrobe as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Strobe

Attribute.Name: StrobeModePulse
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode pulse. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a Pulsing Strobe Mode. When using this StrobeModePulse as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModePulse as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Pulse

Attribute.Name: StrobeModePulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode opening pulse. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a mode where the shutter pulses with slower opening and faster closing behavior. When using this StrobeModePulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModePulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Pulse Open

Attribute.Name: StrobeModePulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode closing pulse. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a mode where the shutter pulses with slower closing and faster opening behavior. When using this StrobeModePulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModePulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Pulse Close

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeRandom
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode random strobe. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a mode where the shutter into a mode of random strobing. When using this StrobeModeRandom as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModeRandom as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Random

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeRandomPulse
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode random pulse. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a mode where the shutter into a mode of random pulse based strobing. When using this StrobeModeRandomPulse as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModeRandomPulse as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Random Pulse

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeRandomPulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode random opening pulse. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a mode where the shutter pulses in random intervals with slower opening and faster closing behavior. When using this StrobeModeRandomPulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModeRandomPulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Random Pulse Open

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeRandomPulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode random closing pulse. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to shutter/strobe into a mode where the shutter pulses in random intervals with slower closing and faster opening behavior. When using this StrobeModeRandomPulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModeRandomPulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Random Pulse Close

Attribute.Name: StrobeModeEffect
Attribute.Definition: Strobe mode random shutter effect feature. Use this attribute together with StrobeFrequency to define the type of the shutter / strobe.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this StrobeModeEffect as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to StrobeModeShutter. In case that StrobeModeShutter already exists in the same geometry, you can use the StrobeModeEffect as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode Effect

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture´s mechanical or electronical shutter feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Open/close style of change of intensity or brightness. Physical values go between 0 = closed, to 1 = open. If there is more than one shutter the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which shutter will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Shutter

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)Strobe
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical strobe shutter feature.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)Strobe as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)Strobe as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the duration of the on time of the strobe.
This defines the offset of the end of the strobe from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobePulse
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical pulse shutter feature.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobePulse as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobePulse as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Pulse

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the pulse is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the pulse from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobePulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical closing pulse shutter feature. The pulse is described by a ramp function.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobePulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobePulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Pulse Close

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the ramp is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the ramp from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobePulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical opening pulse shutter feature. The pulse is described by a ramp function.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobePulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobePulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Pulse Open

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the ramp is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the ramp from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobeRandom
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical random strobe shutter feature.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobeRandom as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobeRandom as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Random

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the duration of the on time of the strobe.

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulse
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical random pulse shutter feature.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulse as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulse as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Random Pulse

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical random closing pulse shutter feature. The pulse is described by a ramp function.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Random Pulse Close

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical random opening pulse shutter feature. The pulse is described by a ramp function.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobeRandomPulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Random Pulse Open

Attribute.Name: Shutter(n)StrobeEffect
Attribute.Definition: Controls the frequency of the fixture´s mechanical or electronical shutter effect feature.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Shutter(n)StrobeEffect as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Shutter(n). In case that Shutter(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Shutter(n)StrobeEffect as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Strobe Effect

Attribute.Name: Iris
Attribute.Definition: Controls the diameter of the fixture's beam.
Attribute.Explanation: An iris diaphragm made of movable leaves which allows to gradually change the diameter of the beam is described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris

Attribute.Name: IrisStrobe
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of the iris's strobe feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Strobing function created by fast movements of an iris is described with this attribute. When using this IrisStrobe as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Iris. In case that Iris already exists in the same geometry, you can use the IrisStrobe as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris Strobe

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the duration of the on time of the iris.
This defines the offset of the end of the strobe from the start as percentage of the total period.
This defines the minimum percentage to which the iris closes.

Attribute.Name: IrisStrobeRandom
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of the iris's random movement.
Attribute.Explanation: Strobing in random intervals created by fast movements of an iris is described with this attribute. When using this IrisStrobeRandom as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Iris. In case that Iris already exists in the same geometry, you can use the IrisStrobeRandom as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris Strobe Random

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the duration of the on time of the iris.
This defines the minimum percentage to which the iris closes.

Attribute.Name: IrisPulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of iris's closing pulse.
Attribute.Explanation: A function that allows to adjust frequency of an iris based strobing where the iris pulses with slower closing and faster opening behavior. When using this IrisPulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Iris. In case that Iris already exists in the same geometry, you can use the IrisPulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris Pulse Close

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the pulse of the iris is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the pulse from the start as percentage of the total period.
This defines the minimum percentage to which the iris closes.

Attribute.Name: IrisPulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of iris's opening pulse.
Attribute.Explanation: A function that allows to adjust frequency of an iris based strobing where the iris pulses with slower opening and faster closing behavior. When using this IrisPulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Iris. In case that Iris already exists in the same geometry, you can use the IrisPulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris Pulse Open

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the pulse of the iris is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the pulse from the start as percentage of the total period.
This defines the minimum percentage to which the iris closes.

Attribute.Name: IrisRandomPulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of iris's random closing pulse.
Attribute.Explanation: A function that allows to enable an iris based strobing in random intervals where the iris pulses with slower opening and faster closing behavior. When using this IrisRandomPulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Iris. In case that Iris already exists in the same geometry, you can use the IrisRandomPulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris Random Pulse Close

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the minimum percentage to which the iris closes.

Attribute.Name: IrisRandomPulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of iris's random opening pulse.
Attribute.Explanation: A function that allows to enable an iris based strobing in random intervals where the iris pulses with slower closing and faster opening behavior. When using this IrisRandomPulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Iris. In case that Iris already exists in the same geometry, you can use the IrisRandomPulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Iris Random Pulse Open

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the minimum percentage to which the iris closes.

Attribute.Name: Frost(n)
Attribute.Definition: The ability to soften the fixture's spot light with a frosted lens.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow to control insertion of frost lens or flag into the beam, to soften it, use this attribute to describe this function.
Attribute.Label: Frost

Attribute.Name: Frost(n)PulseOpen
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of frost's opening pulse
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to frost into a mode where the frost pulses with slower opening and faster closing behavior. When using this Frost(n)PulseOpen as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Frost(n). In case that Frost(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Frost(n)PulseOpen as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Frost Pulse Open

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the pulse of the frost is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the pulse from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Frost(n)PulseClose
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of frost's closing pulse.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function that allows to set to frost into a mode where the frost pulses with slower closing and faster opening behavior. When using this Frost(n)PulseClose as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Frost(n). In case that Frost(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Frost(n)PulseClose as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Frost Pulse Close

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the pulse of the frost is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the pulse from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Frost(n)Ramp
Attribute.Definition: Sets frequency of frost's ramp.
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Frost(n)Ramp as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Frost(n). In case that Frost(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Frost(n)Ramp as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Frost Ramp

SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the fraction of one period in which the ramp of the frost is on.
This defines the offset of the end of the ramp from the start as percentage of the total period.

Attribute.Name: Prism(n)
Attribute.Definition: The fixture’s prism wheel (n). Selects prisms in prism wheel (n). A different channel function sets the angle of the indexed position in the selected prism or the angular speed of its continuous rotation. This is the main attribute of prism wheel’s (n) wheel control.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to select a prism in a prism wheel for fixtures that provide prism selection on one channel and prism rotation/indexing on another channel(s). If there is more than one prism wheel or carousel, the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Prism

Attribute.Name: Prism(n)SelectSpin
Attribute.Definition: Selects prisms whose rotation is continuous in prism wheel (n) and controls the angular speed of the prism’s spin within the same channel function.
Attribute.Explanation: Selects prism whose rotation is continuous in prism wheel (n) and controls the angular speed of the prism’s spin within the same channel function. If there is more than one prism wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Prism(n)SelectSpin as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Prism(n). In case that Prism(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Prism(n)SelectSpin as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Prism Select Spin

Attribute.Name: Prism(n)Macro
Attribute.Definition: Macro functions of prism wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute for manufacturer defined fixture specific effects on a prisms. If there is more than one prism wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Prism(n)Macro as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Prism(n). In case that Prism(n) already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Prism(n)Macro as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Prism Macro

Attribute.Name: Prism(n)Pos
Attribute.Definition: Controls angle of indexed rotation of prisms in prism wheel (n). This is the main attribute of prism wheel’s 1 wheel slot control.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set indexed position (angle) of a prism in a prism wheel and is typically used together with Prism(n) attribute which selects the prism. If there is more than one prism wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Prism Pos

Attribute.Name: Prism(n)PosRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls the speed and direction of continuous rotation of prisms in prism wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set speed and direction of continuous rotation of a prism in a prism wheel and is typically used together with Prism(n) attribute which selects the prism. If there is more than one prism wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled. When using this Prism(n)PosRotate as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Prism(n)Pos. In case that Prism(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Prism(n)PosRotate as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Prism Pos Rotate

Attribute.Name: Effects(n)
Attribute.Definition: Generically predefined macros and effects of a fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe some effects and macros that are not possible to describe with some specific attribute.
Attribute.Label: Effects

Attribute.Name: Effects(n)Rate
Attribute.Definition: Frequency of running effects.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set frequency of some unspecified, generic effects.
Attribute.Label: Effects Rate

Attribute.Name: Effects(n)Fade
Attribute.Definition: Snapping or smooth look of running effects.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set fade of some unspecified, generic effects.
Attribute.Label: Effects Fade

Attribute.Name: Effects(n)Adjust(m)
Attribute.Definition: Controls parameter (m) of effect (n)
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to set some parameter of some generic effect.
Attribute.Label: Effects Adjust

Attribute.Name: Effects(n)Pos
Attribute.Definition: Controls angle of indexed rotation of slot/effect in effect wheel/macro (n). This is the main attribute of effect wheel/macro (n) slot/effect control.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to set angle parameter of some generic effect.
Attribute.Label: Effects Pos

Attribute.Name: Effects(n)PosRotate
Attribute.Definition: Controls speed and direction of slot/effect in effect wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: When using this Effects(n)PosRotate as a Channel Function attribute, make sure that the attribute for the Logical Channel is set to Effects(n)Pos. In case that Effects(n)Pos already exists in the same geometry, you can use the Effects(n)PosRotate as the Logical Channel attribute.
Attribute.Label: Effects Pos Rotate

Attribute.Name: EffectsSync
Attribute.Definition: Sets offset between running effects and effects 2.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe offsets between some generic effects.
Attribute.Label: Effects Sync

Attribute.Name: BeamShaper
Attribute.Definition: Activates fixture's beam shaper.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe beam shaper function of the device.
Attribute.Label: Beam Shaper
