SubPhysicalUnit.Description: This defines the size of the beam compared to the original size.
This defines the size of the beam compared to the original size.

Attribute.Name: BeamShaperMacro
Attribute.Definition: Predefined presets for fixture's beam shaper positions.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe predefined macros of the beam shaper function of the device.
Attribute.Label: Beam Shaper Macro

Attribute.Name: BeamShaperPos
Attribute.Definition: Indexing of fixture's beam shaper.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe indexing of the beam shaper function of the device.
Attribute.Label: Beam Shaper Pos

Attribute.Name: BeamShaperPosRotate
Attribute.Definition: Continuous rotation of fixture's beam shaper.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe spinning/rotation of the beam shaper function of the device.
Attribute.Label: Beam Shaper Pos Rotate

Attribute.Name: Zoom
Attribute.Definition: Controls the spread of the fixture's beam/spot.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is used to adjust the beam angle of the light source.
Attribute.Label: Zoom

Attribute.Name: ZoomModeSpot
Attribute.Definition: Selects spot mode of zoom.
Attribute.Explanation: On devices with a Zoom which can be set to various types of projections, use this attribute to describe a Zoom mode with a flat projection field definition.
Attribute.Label: Zoom Mode Spot

Attribute.Name: ZoomModeBeam
Attribute.Definition: Selects beam mode of zoom.
Attribute.Explanation: On devices with a Zoom which can be set to various types of projections, use this attribute to describe a Zoom mode with a beamy, hot-spoty type of projection.
Attribute.Label: Zoom Mode Beam

Attribute.Name: DigitalZoom
Attribute.Definition: Controls the image size within the defined projection. Used on digital projection based devices
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe a zoom of for example a digital projector, where the image changes size but the beam angle remains the same.
Attribute.Label: Digital Zoom

Attribute.Name: Focus(n)
Attribute.Definition: Controls the sharpness of the fixture's spot light. Can blur or sharpen the edge of the spot.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe the sharpening/focusing function of the device. Some may call it the Edge.
Attribute.Label: Focus

Attribute.Name: Focus(n)Adjust
Attribute.Definition: Autofocuses functionality using presets.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function which sets some parameters or behaviors that make the optical system of the device to automatically keep the projection focused.
Attribute.Label: Focus Adjust

Attribute.Name: Focus(n)Distance
Attribute.Definition: Autofocuses functionality using distance.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function which sets a distance at which the optical system of the device will automatically keep the projection focused.
Attribute.Label: Focus Distance

Attribute.Name: Control(n)
Attribute.Definition: Controls the channel of a fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute do describe a function (typically a DMX channel) which provides access to settings and to controls allowing changes of the behavior of the device, like striking and dousing of the lamp and so on. These individual settings or for example lamp controls are then described by their specific attributes. If there is more than one control channel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which control channel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Control

Attribute.Name: DimmerMode
Attribute.Definition: Selects different modes of intensity.
Attribute.Explanation: Switching between different types of dimming modes is described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Dimmer Mode

Attribute.Name: DimmerCurve
Attribute.Definition: Selects different dimmer curves of the fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: Switching between different types of dimming curves provided by the device is described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Dimmer Curve

Attribute.Name: BlackoutMode
Attribute.Definition: Close the light output under certain conditions (movement correction, gobo movement, etc.).
Attribute.Explanation: If the device provides a way to disable light output when gobo, color or other elements are moving or when for example movement position is being restored after external manual intervention, use this attribute to describe this behavior/settings.
Attribute.Label: Blackout Mode

Attribute.Name: LEDFrequency
Attribute.Definition: Controls LED frequency.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function which allows to change frequency of LEDs.
Attribute.Label: LED Frequency

Attribute.Name: LEDZoneMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes zones of LEDs.
Attribute.Explanation: For multipixel LED devices that allow to choose which zones of LEDs are being used, use this attribute to describe this function.
Attribute.Label: LED Zone Mode

Attribute.Name: PixelMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls behavior of LED pixels.
Attribute.Explanation: For multipixel devices that allow to set behavior of pixel control (for example order of LEDs), use this attribute to describe such behavior/settings.
Attribute.Label: Pixel Mode

Attribute.Name: PanMode
Attribute.Definition: Selects fixture's pan mode. Selects between a limited pan range (e.g. -270 to 270) or a continuous pan range.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow different modes of pan behavior, use this attribute to describe the behavior.
Attribute.Label: Pan Mode

Attribute.Name: TiltMode
Attribute.Definition: Selects fixture's pan mode. Selects between a limited tilt range (e.g. -130 to 130) or a continuous tilt range.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow different modes of tilt behavior, use this attribute to describe the behavior.
Attribute.Label: Tilt Mode

Attribute.Name: PanTiltMode
Attribute.Definition: Selects fixture's pan/tilt mode. Selects between a limited pan/tilt range or a continuous pan/tilt range.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow different modes of behavior of pan and tilt, use this attribute to describe the behavior.
Attribute.Label: Pan Tilt Mode

Attribute.Name: PositionModes
Attribute.Definition: Selects the fixture's position mode.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow different modes of behavior of their positioning system, use this attribute to describe the behavior.
Attribute.Label: Position Modes

Attribute.Name: Gobo(n)WheelMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes control between selecting, indexing, and rotating the gobos of gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes selecting, indexing, and rotating of gobos in a gobo wheel (n).
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Mode

Attribute.Name: GoboWheelShortcutMode
Attribute.Definition: Defines whether the gobo wheel takes the shortest distance between two positions.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes whether the gobo wheel takes the shortest distance between two positions.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Shortcut Mode

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheel(n)Mode
Attribute.Definition: Changes control between selecting, indexing, and rotating the slots of animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes selecting, indexing, and rotating of slots in an animation wheel (n).
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Mode

Attribute.Name: AnimationWheelShortcutMode
Attribute.Definition: Defines whether the animation wheel takes the shortest distance between two positions.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes whether the animation wheel takes the shortest distance between two positions.
Attribute.Label: Animation Wheel Shortcut Mode

Attribute.Name: Color(n)Mode
Attribute.Definition: Changes control between selecting, continuous selection, half selection, random selection, color spinning, etc. in colors of color wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes between selecting, continuous selection, half selection, random selection, color spinning, etc. of colors in a color wheel (n).
Attribute.Label: Color Mode

Attribute.Name: ColorWheelShortcutMode
Attribute.Definition: Defines whether the color wheel takes the shortest distance between two colors.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes whether the color wheel takes the shortest distance between two colors.
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Shortcut Mode

Attribute.Name: CyanMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls how Cyan is used within the fixture's cyan CMY-mixing feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe the mode of operation of the Cyan.
Attribute.Label: Cyan Mode

Attribute.Name: MagentaMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls how Magenta is used within the fixture's magenta CMY-mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe the mode of operation of the Magenta.
Attribute.Label: Magenta Mode

Attribute.Name: YellowMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls how Yellow is used within the fixture's yellow CMY-mixing feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe the mode of operation of the Yellow.
Attribute.Label: Yellow Mode

Attribute.Name: ColorMixMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes control between selecting continuous selection, half selection, random selection, color spinning, etc. in color mixing.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices, that allow setting of mode of operation or relationship of elements of the color mixing system of the fixture, for example color wheel selection modes, or additive/subtractive/... relationship of color mixing systems within the fixture.
Attribute.Label: Color Mix Mode

Attribute.Name: ChromaticMode
Attribute.Definition: Selects chromatic behavior of the device.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that defines if CCT related channels (CTC, CTO, CTB...) influence only white colors or whether CCT is applied also to mixed colors.
Attribute.Label: Chromatic Mode

Attribute.Name: ColorCalibrationMode
Attribute.Definition: Sets calibration mode (for example on/off).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a feature that enable or disable color calibrations of the device.
Attribute.Label: Color Calibration Mode

Attribute.Name: ColorConsistency
Attribute.Definition: Controls consistent behavior of color.
Attribute.Explanation: Some devices allow to eliminate inconsistencies of color mixing output due to intensity drop caused by thermal management. In devices that allow to enable/disable internal color consistency setting, use this attribute to describe a such function.
Attribute.Label: Color Consistency

Attribute.Name: ColorControl
Attribute.Definition: Controls special color related functions.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe some color related control functions of the device.
Attribute.Label: Color Control

Attribute.Name: ColorModelMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls color model (CMY/RGB/HSV...).
Attribute.Explanation: In devices that have a function to allow switching between various ways of color mixing (CMY, RGB, HSV...), this attribute is used to describe this function.
Attribute.Label: Color Model Mode

Attribute.Name: ColorSettingsReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets settings of color control channel.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function which allows to set various color related settings back to the default state.
Attribute.Label: Color Settings Reset

Attribute.Name: ColorUniformity
Attribute.Definition: Controls behavior of color uniformity.
Attribute.Explanation: In devices that allow to set a color uniformity between multiple fixtures use this attribute to describe a such function.
Attribute.Label: Color Uniformity

Attribute.Name: CRIMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls CRI settings of output.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to choose a color mixing mode which is prioritizing higher Color Rendering Index.
Attribute.Label: CRI Mode

Attribute.Name: CustomColor
Attribute.Definition: Custom color related functions (save, recall..).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to save a custom color into the lighting device.
Attribute.Label: Custom Color

Attribute.Name: UVStability
Attribute.Definition: Settings for UV stability color behavior.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to choose a color mixing mode which is prioritizing color stability and suppresses UV color.
Attribute.Label: UV Stability

Attribute.Name: WaveLengthCorrection
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to adjust color mixing according to corresponding wavelengths in order to achieve same colors from multiple fixtures.
Attribute.Label: Wave Length Correction

Attribute.Name: WhiteCount
Attribute.Definition: Controls if White LED is proportionally added to RGB.
Attribute.Explanation: Some devices allow internal automatic intensity control of White LED by the device, based on proportions of intensity of remaining LED emitters. Use this attribute to describe this behavior.
Attribute.Label: White Count

Attribute.Name: StrobeMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes strobe style - strobe, pulse, random strobe, etc. - of the shutter attribute.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe strobe, pulse, random strobe and other modes of the shutter attribute.
Attribute.Label: Strobe Mode

Attribute.Name: ZoomMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes modes of the fixture´s zoom.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe the possibility to set different zoom modes, like beam mode or a spot mode.
Attribute.Label: Zoom Mode

Attribute.Name: FocusMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes modes of the fixture’s focus - manual or auto- focus.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe a function that allows to change the mode of the focus.
Attribute.Label: Focus Mode

Attribute.Name: IrisMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes modes of the fixture’s iris - linear, strobe, pulse.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe a function that allows to change the mode of the iris.
Attribute.Label: Iris Mode

Attribute.Name: FanMode
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to switch between different types of fan control modes (for example auto, high, silent).
Attribute.Label: Fan Mode

Attribute.Name: FollowSpotMode
Attribute.Definition: Selects follow spot control mode.
Attribute.Explanation: For fixtures that allow remote enabling/disabling various types of follow spot related features, use this attribute to describe the behavior.
Attribute.Label: Follow Spot Mode

Attribute.Name: BeamEffectIndexRotateMode
Attribute.Definition: Changes mode to control either index or rotation of the beam effects.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe a function that allows to change the mode of operation of beam effects.
Attribute.Label: Beam Effect Index Rotate Mode

Attribute.Name: IntensityMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's intensity.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of dimming behavior.
Attribute.Label: Intensity Speed

Attribute.Name: PositionMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's pan/tilt.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement for pan/tilt.
Attribute.Label: Position Speed

Attribute.Name: ColorMixMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's ColorMix presets.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement for color mixing elements.
Attribute.Label: Color Mix Speed

Attribute.Name: ColorWheelSelectMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's color wheel.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of color wheel(s).
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Select Speed

Attribute.Name: GoboWheel(n)MSpeed
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of color wheel(s). If there is more than one gobo wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Speed

Attribute.Name: IrisMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's iris.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of iris(s).
Attribute.Label: Iris Speed

Attribute.Name: Prism(n)MSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's prism wheel (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of prism(s). If there is more than one prism or prism wheel the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which prism/wheel will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Prism Speed

Attribute.Name: FocusMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's focus.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of focus.
Attribute.Label: Focus Speed

Attribute.Name: Frost(n)MSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's frost (n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of frost. If there is more than one frost the wild-card (n) will be incremented and the number then indicates which frost will be controlled.
Attribute.Label: Frost Speed

Attribute.Name: ZoomMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's zoom.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of zoom.
Attribute.Label: Zoom Speed

Attribute.Name: FrameMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's shapers.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement of framing.
Attribute.Label: Frame Speed

Attribute.Name: GlobalMSpeed
Attribute.Definition: General speed of fixture's features.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to set a speed of movement which is used on elements of the device.
Attribute.Label: Global M Speed

Attribute.Name: ReflectorAdjust
Attribute.Definition: Movement speed of the fixture's frost.
Attribute.Explanation: Adjustment of the reflector of the device (for example setting it to a hot-spot output or a flat-field output) is described with this attribute.
Attribute.Label: Reflector Adjust

Attribute.Name: FixtureGlobalReset
Attribute.Definition: Generally resets the entire fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger a complete internal reset of the fixture.
Attribute.Label: Fixture Global Reset

Attribute.Name: DimmerReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's dimmer.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's dimmer, dimmer flags or other dimming elements.
Attribute.Label: Dimmer Reset

Attribute.Name: ShutterReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's shutter.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's shutter flags or elements.
Attribute.Label: Shutter Reset

Attribute.Name: BeamReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's beam features.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's beam lenses or elements.
Attribute.Label: Beam Reset

Attribute.Name: ColorMixReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's color mixing system.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's color mixing system.
Attribute.Label: Color Mix Reset

Attribute.Name: ColorWheelReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's color wheel.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's color wheels.
Attribute.Label: Color Wheel Reset

Attribute.Name: FocusReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's focus.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's framing shutter or focus lenses or elements.
Attribute.Label: Focus Reset

Attribute.Name: FrameReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's shapers.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's framing shutter or framing elements.
Attribute.Label: Frame Reset

Attribute.Name: GoboWheelReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's gobo wheel.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's gobo wheels.
Attribute.Label: Gobo Wheel Reset

Attribute.Name: IntensityReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's intensity.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's intensity
Attribute.Label: Intensity Reset

Attribute.Name: IrisReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's iris.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's iris.
Attribute.Label: Iris Reset

Attribute.Name: PositionReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's pan/tilt.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's positional system
Attribute.Label: Position Reset

Attribute.Name: PanReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's pan.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's pan.
Attribute.Label: Pan Reset

Attribute.Name: TiltReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's tilt.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's tilt.
Attribute.Label: Tilt Reset

Attribute.Name: ZoomReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's zoom.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's zoom lenses or elements.
Attribute.Label: Zoom Reset

Attribute.Name: CTBReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's CTB.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's CTB flags or elements.
Attribute.Label: CTB Reset

Attribute.Name: CTOReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's CTO.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's CTO flags or elements.
Attribute.Label: CTO Reset

Attribute.Name: CTCReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's CTC.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's CTC flags or elements.
Attribute.Label: CTC Reset

Attribute.Name: AnimationSystemReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's animation system features.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's animation wheels or elements.
Attribute.Label: Animation System Reset

Attribute.Name: FixtureCalibrationReset
Attribute.Definition: Resets the fixture's calibration.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute is describing a function that allows the operator to trigger an internal reset of the fixture's calibrations.
Attribute.Label: Fixture Calibration Reset

Attribute.Name: Function
Attribute.Definition: Generally controls features of the fixture.
Attribute.Explanation: If you cannot find other attribute that would describe a feature of the Power/Control channel, use this generic attribute.
Attribute.Label: Function

Attribute.Name: LampControl
Attribute.Definition: Controls the fixture's lamp on/lamp off feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe functionality that allows to strike and douse the lamp of the fixture.
Attribute.Label: Lamp Control

Attribute.Name: DisplayIntensity
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts intensity of display
Attribute.Explanation: For fixtures with the possibility to adjust the brightness of their menu display screen, use this attribute to describe such functionality.
Attribute.Label: Display Intensity

Attribute.Name: DMXInput
Attribute.Definition: Selects DMX Input
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe behavior that allows changing the source of the DMX signal.
Attribute.Label: DMX Input

Attribute.Name: NoFeature
Attribute.Definition: Ranges without a functionality.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute for functions that do not define any functions. They might be for example reserved for later use.
Attribute.Label: No Feature

Attribute.Name: Dummy
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute for functions that do not define any functions. They might be for example reserved for later use.
Attribute.Label: Dummy

Attribute.Name: Blower(n)
Attribute.Definition: Fog or hazer‘s blower feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a blower function.
Attribute.Label: Blower

Attribute.Name: Fan(n)
Attribute.Definition: Fog or hazer's Fan feature.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this to describe a function that controls a fan of a device.
Attribute.Label: Fan

Attribute.Name: Fog(n)
Attribute.Definition: Fog or hazer's Fog feature.
Attribute.Explanation: A function that controls fog/haze function of a fog machine.
Attribute.Label: Fog

Attribute.Name: Haze(n)
Attribute.Definition: Fog or hazer's Haze feature.
Attribute.Explanation: A function that controls fog/haze function of a haze machine.
Attribute.Label: Haze

Attribute.Name: LampPowerMode
Attribute.Definition: Controls the energy consumption of the lamp.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow to set different lamp modes (Low Power mode, Eco mode...), use this attribute to describe this function.
Attribute.Label: Lamp Power Mode

Attribute.Name: Fans
Attribute.Definition: Controls a fixture or device fan.
Attribute.Explanation: For devices that allow to set a fan speed, use this attribute to describe this function.
Attribute.Label: Fans

Attribute.Name: Blade(n)A
Attribute.Definition: 1 of 2 shutters that shape the top/right/bottom/left of the beam.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe framing shutters or barndoors of the device. When only the Blade(n)A is used, it is controlling insertion of the complete framing shutter by moving the long edge. Eventual rotation of this edge is controlled by Blade(n)Rot attribute. If both Blade(n)A and Blade(n)B are used, the Blade(n)A is controlling one corner and the Blade(n)B is controlling the second corner of the framing shutter, thus together these attributes are controlling the insertion and rotation at the same time. Use the (n) enumerator to set correct blade - when looking at the projection, 1 is bottom (from positive to negative values in Y axis), 2 is left (from negative to positive values on the X axis), 3 is top (from negative to positive values on the Y axis) and 4 is right (from positive to negative values on the X axis). (When looking into the device lens, 1 is top, 2 is left, 3 is bottom, 4 is right). The physical values describe the amount of insertion, where at 0 the blade is completely out of the beam and at 1 the blade is fully covering the diameter of the beam.
Attribute.Label: Blade A

Attribute.Name: Blade(n)B
Attribute.Definition: 2 of 2 shutters that shape the top/right/bottom/left of the beam.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe framing shutters or barndoors of the device in which the framing shutter is controlled by adjusting it's corners. This Blade(n)B is used in conjunction with the Blade(n)A attribute, where the Blade(n)A is controlling one corner and the Blade(n)B is controlling the second corner of the framing shutter, thus together these attributes are controlling the insertion and rotation at the same time. Use the (n) enumerator to set correct blade - when looking at the projection, 1 is bottom (from positive to negative values in Y axis), 2 is left (from negative to positive values on the X axis), 3 is top (from negative to positive values on the Y axis) and 4 is right (from positive to negative values on the X axis). (When looking into the device lens, 1 is top, 2 is left, 3 is bottom, 4 is right). The physical values describe the amount of insertion, where at 0 the blade is completely out of the beam and at 1 the blade is fully covering the diameter of the beam.
Attribute.Label: Blade B

Attribute.Name: Blade(n)Rot
Attribute.Definition: Rotates position of blade(n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe rotation of the blades of framing shutters or barndoors. Use the (n) enumerator to set correct blade, when looking at the projection, 1 is bottom, 2 is left, 3 is top and 4 is right. (When looking into the device lens, 1 is top, 2 is left, 3 is bottom, 4 is right).
Attribute.Label: Blade Rot

Attribute.Name: ShaperRot
Attribute.Definition: Rotates position of blade assembly.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows you to describe a function which rotates the complete carousel of framing shutters or barndors.
Attribute.Label: Shaper Rot

Attribute.Name: ShaperMacros
Attribute.Definition: Predefined presets for shaper positions.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows for selecting a fixture's predefined macro functions for the Blades/Shapers.
Attribute.Label: Shaper Macros

Attribute.Name: ShaperMacrosSpeed
Attribute.Definition: Speed of predefined effects on shapers.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to control the speed of effects of shapers
Attribute.Label: Shaper Macros Speed

Attribute.Name: BladeSoft(n)A
Attribute.Definition: 1 of 2 soft edge blades that shape the top/right/bottom/left of the beam.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe soft edge framing shutters or barndoors of the device. When only the Blade(n)A is used, it is controlling insertion of the complete framing shutter by moving the long edge. If both Blade(n)A and Blade(n)B are used, the Blade(n)A is controlling one corner and the Blade(n)B is controlling the second corner of the framing shutter. Use the (n) enumerator to set correct blade - when looking at the projection, 1 is bottom (from positive to negative values in Y axis), 2 is left (from negative to positive values on the X axis), 3 is top (from negative to positive values on the Y axis) and 4 is right (from positive to negative values on the X axis). (When looking into the device lens, 1 is top, 2 is left, 3 is bottom, 4 is right). The physical values describe the amount of insertion, where at 0 the blade is completely out of the beam and at 1 the blade is fully covering the diameter of the beam.
Attribute.Label: Blade Soft A

Attribute.Name: BladeSoft(n)B
Attribute.Definition: 2 of 2 soft edge blades that shape the top/right/bottom/left of the beam.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe soft edge framing shutters or barndoors of the device in which the framing shutter in controlled by adjusting it's corners. This Blade(n)B is used in conjunction with the Blade(n)A attribute, where the Blade(n)A is controlling one corner and the Blade(n)B is controlling the second corner of the framing shutter. Use the (n) enumerator to set correct blade - when looking at the projection, 1 is bottom (from positive to negative values in Y axis), 2 is left (from negative to positive values on the X axis), 3 is top (from negative to positive values on the Y axis) and 4 is right (from positive to negative values on the X axis). (When looking into the device lens, 1 is top, 2 is left, 3 is bottom, 4 is right). The physical values describe the amount of insertion, where at 0 the blade is completely out of the beam and at 1 the blade is fully covering the diameter of the beam.
Attribute.Label: Blade Soft B

Attribute.Name: KeyStone(n)A
Attribute.Definition: 1 of 2 corners that shape the top/right/bottom/left of the beam.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes a behavior typically used on video projection, to adjust parallax correction.
Attribute.Label: Key Stone A

Attribute.Name: KeyStone(n)B
Attribute.Definition: 2 of 2 corners that shape the top/right/bottom/left of the beam.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute describes a behavior typically used on video projection, to adjust parallax correction.
Attribute.Label: Key Stone B

Attribute.Name: Video
Attribute.Definition: Controls video features.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe generic functions related to video controls.
Attribute.Label: Video

Attribute.Name: VideoEffect(n)Type
Attribute.Definition: Selects dedicated effects which are used for media.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe generic video effects.
Attribute.Label: Video Effect Type

Attribute.Name: VideoEffect(n)Parameter(m)
Attribute.Definition: Controls parameter (m) of VideoEffect(n)Type.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe generic video effects parameters.
Attribute.Label: Video Effect Parameter

Attribute.Name: VideoCamera(n)
Attribute.Definition: Selects the video camera(n).
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe features related to a video camera.
Attribute.Label: Video Camera

Attribute.Name: FieldOfView
Attribute.Definition: Defines field of view.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe features controlling field of view of video projection.
Attribute.Label: Field Of View

Attribute.Name: InputSource
Attribute.Definition: Defines media input source e.g. a camera input.
Attribute.Explanation: This attribute allows to set a video input source.
Attribute.Label: Input Source

Attribute.Name: VideoBlendMode
Attribute.Definition: Defines mode of video blending.
Attribute.Explanation: Use this attribute to describe a function that allows to choose a mode of blending of a video output.
Attribute.Label: Video Blend Mode

Attribute.Name: VideoSoundVolume(n)
Attribute.Definition: Adjusts sound volume.
Attribute.Explanation: Describes the audio volume function of a device.
Attribute.Label: Video Sound Volume
